what_it_checks: hermes binary is resolvable on PATH
failure_symptom: All tool/subprocess calls to the host fail immediately
likely_causes: Hermes not installed
PATH not exported in this shell/agent env
fix_steps: Run: which hermes
If missing, install/repair Hermes Agent and ensure its bin dir is on PATH
For agents, confirm the spawned shell inherits PATH
change_robust: Uses shutil.which at runtime — independent of any hardcoded path
detection_method: shutil.which(ctx.hermes_bin) is not None

what_it_checks: hermes --version is parseable (host is a known-good build)
failure_symptom: Unknown host version; contract assumptions may not hold
likely_causes: Hermes build does not print the expected banner
Broken install
fix_steps: Run: hermes --version
Reinstall Hermes if the version banner is missing
change_robust: Parses live --version output, not a pinned constant
detection_method: regex 'Hermes Agent v<ver>' matches `hermes --version`

what_it_checks: host advertises -z/--oneshot (the non-interactive entry every plugin relies on)
failure_symptom: Plugin/CLI falls into interactive mode or errors when invoking the host
likely_causes: A Hermes update renamed/removed the oneshot flag
fix_steps: Run: hermes --help | grep -- -z
If absent, pin to a compatible Hermes version or update the plugin to the new flag
change_robust: Greps live --help output each run — detects a Hermes update that breaks the contract
detection_method: '-z' and '--oneshot' present in `hermes --help`

what_it_checks: this plugin's entry point is registered under hermes_agent.plugins and import+register() works
failure_symptom: Plugin not loaded by Hermes; its tools/subcommand are absent
likely_causes: pip install incomplete
entry point metadata missing
register() import error
fix_steps: Run: python -c "import importlib.metadata as m; print([e.value for e in m.entry_points(group='hermes_agent.plugins') if 'cluxion-hermes-call-cli' in e.name.lower()])"
Reinstall: pip install --force-reinstall cluxion-hermes-call-cli
Confirm the entry module imports and exposes a callable register
change_robust: Reads live importlib.metadata.entry_points(), not a config file
detection_method: entry_points(group='hermes_agent.plugins') contains this dist and register is callable

what_it_checks: `hermes call` subcommand is advertised and returns help (exit 0)
failure_symptom: Delegating via the host subcommand path fails with 'invalid choice'
likely_causes: register_cli_command not called
Hermes CLI registration API changed
fix_steps: Run: hermes call --help
If 'invalid choice', reinstall cluxion-hermes-call-cli and confirm register_cli_command is used
change_robust: Runs live `hermes call --help` — catches host CLI-registration drift
detection_method: `hermes call --help` exits 0

what_it_checks: installed distribution version equals the package __version__
failure_symptom: Stale or mismatched install; fixes/behaviors may not be the deployed ones
likely_causes: Partial upgrade
multiple installs shadowing each other
fix_steps: Run: pip show cluxion-hermes-call-cli
Force a clean reinstall: pip install --force-reinstall cluxion-hermes-call-cli
change_robust: Compares live importlib.metadata.version() to __version__ — no hardcoding
detection_method: importlib.metadata.version('cluxion-hermes-call-cli') == __version__

what_it_checks: the plugin does NOT register a '-p' short flag (it collides with the Hermes host global -p/--profile)
failure_symptom: `hermes call -p "text"` errors: Profile 'text' does not exist (exit 1)
likely_causes: plugin re-registered -p as prompt alias, shadowing host -p/--profile
fix_steps: Ensure cli.py registers only --prompt (long form), not -p
Use the positional PROMPT or --prompt through the host
change_robust: Inspects the argparse option strings at runtime; independent of host version
detection_method: '-p' not in the call parser's option strings

what_it_checks: empty/whitespace prompt is rejected (never forwarded as `hermes -z ""` → interactive TUI block)
failure_symptom: Process blocks on a TTY waiting for interactive input; agent timeout
likely_causes: _resolve_prompt only rejected None
child stdin not DEVNULL
fix_steps: _resolve_prompt must error on not prompt.strip()
Pass stdin=subprocess.DEVNULL to every hermes Popen
change_robust: Calls the resolver with '' and asserts it errors; checks Popen kwargs
detection_method: _resolve_prompt('') raises SystemExit and core Popen uses stdin=DEVNULL

what_it_checks: --ask injects the no-tools honesty preface so the model never claims tool actions it cannot do
failure_symptom: --ask returns confident FALSE success (e.g. 'file created') though nothing happened
likely_causes: ASK_MODE_PREFACE not prepended in ask mode
fix_steps: Prepend ASK_MODE_PREFACE to the prompt when options.ask is true
Document --ask as answer-only/no-tools in help + README
change_robust: Asserts the preface constant is prepended on the ask path
detection_method: ASK_MODE_PREFACE present and applied when ask=True

what_it_checks: 'doctor'/'gc' positional only triggers magic subcommands for a bare invocation (no prompt-shaping flags)
failure_symptom: `hermes call --ask doctor` runs the doctor instead of asking the model
likely_causes: magic routing fired on args.prompt regardless of other flags
fix_steps: Gate doctor/gc routing on absence of ask/prompt_alias/toolsets/json/until_done/sandbox
change_robust: Exercises the routing with --ask present and asserts the model path is taken
detection_method: magic words gated by no-other-flag condition

what_it_checks: Hermes binary is available on PATH or at the specified path
failure_symptom: OSError: failed to start hermes; subprocess.Popen raises 'No such file or directory'; hermes-call answer is empty
likely_causes: Hermes not installed
Hermes installed but not on user's PATH
User specified --hermes-bin path that does not exist
PATH environment variable corrupted or not exported to subprocess
Shell configuration not loading PATH correctly
fix_steps: Verify hermes is installed: run 'which hermes' or 'hermes --version'
If not found, install/reinstall Hermes Agent from https://hermes-agent.nousresearch.com
Verify PATH includes Hermes location: echo $PATH | grep -i hermes or $HOME/.local/bin
If using custom hermes_bin path, ensure path is absolute and file exists: ls -l /path/to/hermes
Test subprocess can see hermes by running in subshell: bash -c 'which hermes'
change_robust: The check _resolve_binary() inspects PATH at runtime via shutil.which(), so it adapts to PATH changes between Hermes updates; robust to env differences
detection_method: shutil.which('hermes') or check Path(hermes_bin).exists() if hermes_bin contains / or \

what_it_checks: Hermes --help output advertises -z/--oneshot and -t/--toolsets flags
failure_symptom: hermes-call exits with returncode 2 when building command; subprocess returns unrecognized argument error; answer is empty
likely_causes: Hermes version is too old (does not have -z/--oneshot flag)
Hermes was downgraded after cluxion-hermes-call-cli was installed
Hermes help output changed format or moved flags to subcommands
hermes binary is not the real Hermes Agent (symlink to wrong binary, wrapper script, mock)
fix_steps: Check Hermes version: hermes --version (should be >= 0.14.0 for -z, >= 0.12.0 for -t)
View actual help: hermes --help | grep -E '(-z|-t|oneshot|toolsets)'
If missing, upgrade Hermes: hermes self-upgrade or reinstall
If symlink: verify target is real Hermes: ls -la $(which hermes) && file $(which hermes)
Run doctor again to confirm: hermes-call doctor
change_robust: Search is substring-based (not regex fragile), checks for both short and long flags; detects help format shifts; robust to minor help text rewording
detection_method: subprocess hermes --help; search for '-z' and '--oneshot' and '-t' and '--toolsets' in combined stdout+stderr

what_it_checks: Hermes has a default model configured in ~/.hermes/config.yaml or $HERMES_HOME/config.yaml
failure_symptom: hermes-call answer field is empty or 'no final response was produced'; Hermes logs 'no default model'; user can still call with -m override
likely_causes: User ran 'hermes logout' or 'hermes auth-reset'
Hermes config.yaml was manually edited and model.default removed
config.yaml file is corrupted (invalid YAML)
User is running in fresh container/VM with no Hermes setup
$HERMES_HOME env var points to non-existent or wrong config
fix_steps: Check config exists: test -f ~/.hermes/config.yaml && echo 'found' || echo 'missing'
View current config: cat ~/.hermes/config.yaml | grep -A 2 'model:'
Set default model: hermes config model.default grok-4.3 (or other available model)
Verify auth is valid: hermes auth list (should show credentials, not 'not authenticated')
If $HERMES_HOME is set, verify it: echo $HERMES_HOME && ls $HERMES_HOME/config.yaml
Run doctor again: hermes-call doctor (should show default model in epilog)
change_robust: Parser reads YAML indentation-aware, handles quoted/unquoted values, null checks; detects when model block exists but is empty; fails gracefully with 'model block not found' reason
detection_method: Read config.yaml and parse model.default and model.provider fields using config._parse_model_block(); verify model value is not empty

what_it_checks: Hermes sessions subcommand and list/export/delete actions are available
failure_symptom: hermes-call cannot capture or cleanup sessions; answer is empty; session IDs are not parsed; session cleanup is skipped with reason 'after_list_failed'
likely_causes: Hermes version too old (sessions command added in v0.12.0)
Hermes downgraded after install
sessions subcommand was removed or renamed in a major Hermes update
User has a custom hermes wrapper that strips the sessions command
fix_steps: Check Hermes version: hermes --version (should be >= 0.12.0)
Verify sessions command exists: hermes sessions --help | head -5
View available actions: hermes sessions --help | grep -E 'list|export|delete'
If missing, upgrade Hermes: hermes self-upgrade
Verify hermes binary is not a wrapper: type hermes && file $(which hermes)
change_robust: Uses word-boundary regex to detect command names, not string contains; resilient to help text rewording; detects missing individual actions (list vs export vs delete)
detection_method: subprocess hermes sessions --help; search for word boundaries around 'list', 'export', 'delete' using _contains_word() regex

what_it_checks: Session IDs can be parsed from 'hermes sessions list' output using the documented regex pattern
failure_symptom: cleanup_created_session returns 'no_new_session'; sessions are not cleaned up; answer includes 'session cleanup skipped'; hermes-call cannot detect its own session
likely_causes: Hermes sessions list table format changed (moved ID position or added columns)
Session IDs format changed (no longer 8dig_6dig_hex)
Hermes sessions list returns JSONL or CSV instead of human table
Output encoding issue (session IDs are valid but regex fails to match)
User's Hermes has custom output formatting via environment variable
fix_steps: Run actual command: hermes sessions list --source cli --limit 20
Check ID format: hermes sessions list --source cli | grep -oE '[0-9]{8}_[0-9]{6}_[0-9a-f]{8,}'
If no output, check there are sessions: hermes sessions list | head
If format changed, report to cluxion-hermes-call-cli: 'hermes sessions list format changed, IDs now...'
Run doctor: hermes-call doctor (output shows parsed count or 'no visible cli sessions')
change_robust: Regex pattern is source-verified against actual Hermes output; handles case where no sessions exist (returns true 'no visible cli sessions'); detects parsing failure directly
detection_method: Run 'hermes sessions list --source cli --limit 20'; extract session IDs with SESSION_ID_AT_EOL_RE (pattern: \d{8}_\d{6}_[0-9a-fA-F]+); verify at least one ID or 'No sessions found' message

what_it_checks: Job sandbox root directory (~/.cluxion_hermes/jobs) is writable and round-trip file I/O succeeds
failure_symptom: hermes-call --sandbox fails with 'sandbox cleanup skipped'; job_dir is not None but job_deleted is False; job directory remains on disk after run
likely_causes: Home directory filesystem is read-only or mounted with nowrite flag
File permissions: jobs_root exists but is mode 000 or owned by different user
Disk is full (ENOSPC)
Filesystem has inode exhaustion
SELinux or AppArmor policy blocks writes to ~/.cluxion_hermes
macOS: full disk access not granted to terminal app
fix_steps: Check directory exists and permissions: ls -ld ~/.cluxion_hermes/jobs
Create directory if missing: mkdir -p ~/.cluxion_hermes/jobs
Fix permissions: chmod 700 ~/.cluxion_hermes/jobs
Test write: touch ~/.cluxion_hermes/jobs/test.txt && rm ~/.cluxion_hermes/jobs/test.txt
Check disk space: df -h $HOME | awk '{print $NF, $4}' (should have free space)
Check filesystem type: df -T $HOME (should not be 'ro' or read-only mount)
On macOS, enable Full Disk Access: System Settings > Privacy & Security > Full Disk Access > Terminal
Run doctor again: hermes-call doctor
change_robust: Probe creates and deletes unique marker file, so detects all I/O failure modes (read, write, delete); adapts to DEFAULT_JOBS_ROOT env variable if set; uses uuid to avoid collisions
detection_method: Create temp marker file at jobs_root/.doctor-<uuid>.marker with JSON payload; read it back; verify content matches; delete marker; catch OSError or write failures

what_it_checks: ASK_TOOLSETS constant is set to 'context_engine', which is the verified no-tool-required toolset mapping for --ask mode
failure_symptom: hermes-call --ask produces answer with tool calls still available; user expects no tools but Hermes shows tool list; live_no_tools doctor check fails
likely_causes: Hermes renamed or removed the 'context_engine' toolset
Hermes' --ask/-t toolset mapping changed in a major version
User set custom HERMES_HOME with different toolset configuration
Hermes version mismatch: cluxion expects old toolset name
fix_steps: Check constant in source: grep -n ASK_TOOLSETS src/cluxion_hermes_call/core.py
Run doctor --live: hermes-call doctor --live (includes live_no_tools check)
Test manually: hermes -t context_engine -z 'List files. If you have no tools, reply NO_TOOLS' (should say NO_TOOLS)
If Hermes shows tools, find the actual no-tool toolset: hermes --help | grep -A 10 'toolsets'
If changed, file issue: 'Hermes renamed no-tool toolset from context_engine to X'
Update ASK_TOOLSETS in core.py and rebuild
change_robust: Static check against hardcoded constant; live --live mode probes actual behavior by inspecting Hermes response for NO_TOOLS marker; detects silently wrong toolset mapping
detection_method: Check hardcoded constant ASK_TOOLSETS in core.py equals 'context_engine'; compare to Hermes' actual no-tool toolset (requires live check with doctor --live)

what_it_checks: Hermes command-line interface for oneshot (-z) and resume (chat --resume) modes matches documented contract
failure_symptom: hermes-call sends command with -z but Hermes rejects 'unrecognized argument'; hermes-call tries 'chat --resume' but Hermes does not recognize flag; exit code 2
likely_causes: Hermes version renamed -z to --oneshot-only or similar
chat subcommand was moved or renamed
--resume flag behavior changed (now called --session or --id)
Hermes major version (2.x) changed CLI structure
fix_steps: Check hermes --help for exact flag names: hermes --help | grep -i 'oneshot\|session'
Check chat subcommand: hermes chat --help | head
Verify available flags: hermes chat --help | grep -i 'resume\|session\|id'
If flags changed, update _build_hermes_command() in core.py
Run doctor to verify: hermes-call doctor
change_robust: Help flag checks are already in doctor; -z test is in hermes_help_flags check; resume path is tested indirectly via session cleanup tests
detection_method: hermes --help | grep -E '(-z|oneshot)'; hermes chat --help | grep resume; verify exit codes are 0; check flags appear in help

what_it_checks: Process timeout is correctly applied and child process is terminated after timeout_seconds
failure_symptom: hermes-call hangs for much longer than --timeout value; process does not exit; exit_code is not 124; process group is not terminated
likely_causes: Hermes is started in a separate process group (start_new_session=True) but SIGTERM not reaching child
Process is stuck in uninterruptible sleep state (I/O wait)
os.killpg() fails silently due to permissions
Timeout is measured incorrectly or deadline calc has clock drift
Child spawns its own daemons that survive killpg
fix_steps: Test timeout: timeout 5 hermes-call --timeout 3 'import time; time.sleep(100)' 2>&1 | grep -i 'timeout\|timed'
Check exit code: ...and echo $?  (should be 124)
Verify SIGTERM is sent: strace -e kill hermes-call --timeout 1 '...' 2>&1 | grep kill
Check for orphan processes: ps aux | grep hermes (should be empty after timeout)
Verify os.killpg() permissions: hermes-call runs as current user, killpg should work on own PID
change_robust: Uses subprocess.Popen with start_new_session=True and os.killpg() to terminate process group; timeout is enforced at communicate() level; returncode 124 is deterministic marker for timeout
detection_method: Run hermes with --timeout 2 on a slow prompt (e.g., 'sleep 10'); measure elapsed time; verify exit code is 124 and stderr contains 'timed out' or 'SIGTERM'

what_it_checks: Session cleanup correctly identifies and deletes exactly one newly-created session without race conditions when multiple concurrent runs exist
failure_symptom: cleanup fails with 'multiple_new_sessions' when only one run occurred; session is not deleted; user sees orphaned sessions after run
likely_causes: Another user or process created a session between before and after capture
Sessions list is not transactional; race window between capture and delete
Hermes sessions list caches stale results
Session ID mismatch due to cwd not being captured correctly
Concurrent hermes-call runs in same cwd both try to delete, first wins
fix_steps: For single-user case: run hermes-call and check session is deleted: hermes sessions list --source cli | wc -l (should decrease)
For concurrent case: use --keep-session to avoid race: hermes-call --keep-session '...' & hermes-call --keep-session '...' &
Check expected_cwd matching: hermes sessions export - | jq '.cwd' (should match $(pwd))
If ambiguous, use session-specific cleanup: hermes sessions delete --yes <id>
Run single sequential calls to verify determinism
change_robust: identify_created_session() compares before/after snapshots; if >1 new ID, attempts to disambiguate by exported cwd; if still ambiguous, returns reason; cleanup is optional (keep_session flag bypasses it)
detection_method: Run two hermes-call instances in parallel; capture session IDs before/after each; verify identify_created_session returns exactly one ID when before/after diff is 1; attempt delete and verify 'Deleted session' in output

what_it_checks: Job sandbox deletion safety gates prevent symlink traversal and path escape attacks
failure_symptom: malicious symlink in jobs_root could cause arbitrary file deletion; shutil.rmtree() follows symlink and deletes target; user's real data is deleted
likely_causes: Attacker creates ~/.cluxion_hermes/jobs/uuid -> /home/user/important/data symlink
Hermes sandbox job is created with traversal: cwd = /tmp/jobs/../../etc
Marker file is in parent dir, symlink check is bypassed
User manually creates symlink in jobs_root before hermes-call runs
fix_steps: Check can_delete_job_dir logic: grep -A 20 'def can_delete_job_dir' src/cluxion_hermes_call/jobs.py
Test symlink protection: cd ~/.cluxion_hermes/jobs && ln -s /tmp/test symlink_uuid && hermes-call --sandbox 'true' 2>&1 (should not delete /tmp/test)
Test path escape: mkdir -p /tmp/escape_test && hermes-call --cwd /tmp/escape_test -p 'test' 2>&1 (cwd should not escape jobs_root)
Verify marker file exists: ls -la ~/.cluxion_hermes/jobs/*/. cluxion_hermes_job (should be present, not symlink)
Run doctor: hermes-call doctor (jobs_root_writable check verifies marker round-trip)
change_robust: Multiple safety gates: symlink check (is_symlink()), path resolve and escape check (_is_strictly_under), marker file presence, marker content validation; fail-closed (returns False and reason on any check failure)
detection_method: Attempt to delete a job_dir that is a symlink or whose parent is not jobs_root; verify can_delete_job_dir() returns False with 'job_dir_is_symlink' or 'path_escape'; verify job_dir is not deleted

what_it_checks: Job marker file integrity prevents deletion of unmarked directories and detects tampered markers
failure_symptom: attacker modifies marker file to make Hermes think job is safe to delete; shutil.rmtree() deletes wrong directory; data loss
likely_causes: Marker file permissions too loose (world-writable)
Attacker modifies .cluxion_hermes_job JSON after job creation
Hermes version changes marker format and old marker is unreadable
Marker file is manually deleted by user
fix_steps: Check marker file permissions: ls -la ~/.cluxion_hermes/jobs/*/.cluxion_hermes_job | awk '{print $1, $NF}'
Check marker content: cat ~/.cluxion_hermes/jobs/*/.cluxion_hermes_job | jq . (should have job_id, pid, pid_create_time)
Verify marker matches directory name: job_id=$(jq -r .job_id ~/.cluxion_hermes/jobs/*/.cluxion_hermes_job) && ls -d ~/.cluxion_hermes/jobs/$job_id
Test marker tampering: manually corrupt JSON and run hermes-call (should skip deletion with 'bad_marker' reason)
Fix bad markers: hermes-call gc (prunes unmarked or old jobs)
change_robust: Marker is JSON with job_id, pid, pid_create_time; checks job_id matches directory name; validates PID is positive; catches JSON decode errors; fails safely if any mismatch
detection_method: Create job with marker, modify marker JSON (job_id mismatch, bad PID, corrupt JSON); call delete_job_dir(); verify returns False with appropriate reason; directory is not deleted

what_it_checks: Session export via 'hermes sessions export' produces valid JSONL with parseable session metadata (id, model, cwd)
failure_symptom: fetch_session_model() returns None when model is set; fetch_session_metadata() returns error='missing_export_record'; session cleanup loses model info
likely_causes: Hermes sessions export changed output format (no longer JSONL or moved fields)
Session export does not include model or cwd in metadata
JSON encoding issue (non-ASCII characters in model name)
Export command failed but returned exit code 0
fix_steps: Test export manually: hermes sessions list --source cli --limit 1 | grep -oE '[0-9]{8}_[0-9]{6}_[0-9a-f]{8,}' | head -1 > /tmp/sid.txt && hermes sessions export - --session-id $(cat /tmp/sid.txt)
Check format: cat /tmp/export.jsonl | head -1 | jq . (should be valid JSON with 'id', 'model', 'cwd')
Verify model field: jq .model < /tmp/export.jsonl (should not be null or empty)
If format changed, update fetch_session_metadata() in sessions.py
Run doctor --live: hermes-call doctor --live (will test live_answer check)
change_robust: Parser is line-by-line JSONL; catches JSON decode errors and logs as 'missing_export_record'; optional fields (model, cwd) are safely None if missing; resilient to new fields
detection_method: Run 'hermes sessions export - --session-id <id>'; parse each line as JSON; verify 'id' field matches requested session; extract 'model' and 'cwd' fields

what_it_checks: Completion markers (TASK_COMPLETE, WORK_REMAINS:...) are correctly parsed from final line of Hermes response
failure_symptom: hermes-call --until-done never detects completion; status remains 'incomplete' even when Hermes says TASK_COMPLETE; max_iterations is hit; session is not cleaned up
likely_causes: Hermes adds trailing whitespace or blank lines after marker
Hermes outputs marker on non-final line (model emits marker mid-response)
Marker is not on the final non-empty line
WORK_REMAINS: prefix is followed by newline instead of on same line
Hermes response is truncated by timeout before final line
fix_steps: Test marker parsing: hermes-call --until-done 'Do some work. End with exactly TASK_COMPLETE on final line.' 2>&1 | tail -5
Check final line: hermes-call --until-done '...' 2>&1 | tail -1 (should be 'TASK_COMPLETE' exactly)
Test WORK_REMAINS: hermes-call --until-done 'List work: WORK_REMAINS: fix bug X' 2>&1 | tail -1
Check marker regex: grep -n 'TASK_COMPLETE_MARKER\|WORK_REMAINS_PREFIX' src/cluxion_hermes_call/core.py
Verify parse logic: _parse_completion_marker() in core.py (strips whitespace, checks last non-empty line)
change_robust: Parser strips trailing whitespace and empty lines; checks only final non-empty line; ignores marker case; detects prefix match for WORK_REMAINS; fails gracefully with status='incomplete' if no marker
detection_method: Run hermes-call --until-done with prompt that ends in 'TASK_COMPLETE' or 'WORK_REMAINS: X'; parse last line of output; verify marker is extracted; status is 'complete' or 'incomplete'

what_it_checks: Session ID is correctly identified for --until-done resume when multiple new sessions are created
failure_symptom: hermes-call --until-done fails to identify session; cleanup returns 'multiple_new_sessions:N'; resume is not attempted; status='incomplete'
likely_causes: Two concurrent hermes-call --until-done in same cwd both create sessions
cwd export fails or is not captured correctly by Hermes
identify_created_session() cannot disambiguate by cwd
select_session_by_exported_cwd() fails to match expected_cwd
fix_steps: Test single sequential: hermes-call --until-done 'task1' && hermes-call --until-done 'task2'
Check session cwd: hermes sessions export - | jq -r 'select(.cwd) | .cwd' | sort | uniq -c
Test concurrent (different cwd): (cd /tmp && hermes-call --until-done 'task1' &) && (cd $HOME && hermes-call --until-done 'task2' &) && wait
If ambiguous, use --keep-session and manual cleanup: hermes-call --until-done --keep-session '...' && hermes sessions delete --yes <id>
Verify expected_cwd is resolved: grep -n 'expected_cwd' src/cluxion_hermes_call/sessions.py
change_robust: Session identification uses before/after snapshots of session list; if multiple new IDs, attempts cwd-based selection using exported metadata; fails safely with reason code if ambiguous
detection_method: Run hermes-call --until-done twice concurrently in same cwd; verify each identifies its own session by cwd matching; attempt resume with correct ID; verify different sessions are resumed

what_it_checks: Model passed via -m flag is valid and recognized by Hermes
failure_symptom: hermes-call answer is empty; exit code is 1; Hermes logs 'no final response was produced' or 'model X not configured'
likely_causes: User passed unsupported model: hermes-call -m 'gpt-999' (no such model)
Model name is typo: 'grok-4.2' instead of 'grok-4.3'
Model is valid but authentication failed (API key invalid or expired)
Model provider is not configured (e.g., model=grok but xai-oauth is not authorized)
fix_steps: List available models: hermes config model.catalog (if enabled) or hermes models (if available)
Check current default: hermes config model.default
Test model: hermes -m grok-4.3 -z 'test' (should return a response)
If model does not exist, use default: hermes-call 'prompt' (no -m flag)
If auth failed, re-authorize: hermes auth login or check ~/.hermes/auth.json
change_robust: Model validation happens in Hermes subprocess, not in hermes-call; exit code and response detection are robust; error is caught and reported in exit_code and answer
detection_method: Run hermes -m <model> -z 'hello' with invalid model; check exit code and stderr for 'unknown model', 'model not found', or 'no final response'

what_it_checks: Working directory specified via -C is accessible and can be changed into
failure_symptom: hermes-call exits with exit code 1; stderr contains 'No such file or directory'; answer is empty
likely_causes: User passed non-existent path: hermes-call -C /nonexistent/path
Path exists but is not a directory: hermes-call -C /etc/passwd
Path is inaccessible (permission denied): hermes-call -C /root
Path is a broken symlink
Path was deleted between argument parse and subprocess.Popen
fix_steps: Verify path exists: test -d /path/to/cwd && echo ok || echo missing
Verify path is accessible: cd /path/to/cwd && pwd (should succeed)
Check permissions: ls -ld /path/to/cwd (should have 'x' for current user)
If symlink, verify target: ls -L /path/to/cwd (should resolve)
Use absolute path: hermes-call -C $(cd /path/to/cwd && pwd) 'prompt'
change_robust: Path is resolved with .resolve(strict=False), so it tolerates missing final components; subprocess.Popen will fail if cwd does not exist; error is caught and exit code is 1
detection_method: subprocess.Popen with cwd=<path> after path.resolve(); if resolve() raises OSError or cwd does not exist, Popen raises FileNotFoundError

what_it_checks: Prompt from stdin (- argument) is read correctly and not empty
failure_symptom: hermes-call exits with code 2; error 'PROMPT is required'; or runs with empty prompt and Hermes fails
likely_causes: stdin is closed or EOF at start (pipe broken)
User piped empty file: echo '' | hermes-call -
stdin is a terminal and user did not type anything (Ctrl+D immediately)
fix_steps: Provide prompt via stdin: echo 'prompt text' | hermes-call -
Or use positional: hermes-call 'prompt text'
Or use --prompt: hermes-call --prompt 'prompt text'
If piping from file: cat file.txt | hermes-call - (file must be non-empty)
change_robust: stdin.read() is robust; _resolve_prompt() checks for None and raises parser.error(); empty prompt is treated as falsy and rejected
detection_method: Run hermes-call - with stdin '<empty>'; verify error message 'PROMPT is required'; exit code 2

what_it_checks: JSON output from --json flag is valid and parseable
failure_symptom: stdout contains invalid JSON; json.loads() raises JSONDecodeError; downstream tools cannot parse result
likely_causes: Hermes answer contains unescaped quotes or control characters that break JSON string encoding
json.dumps() encodes non-ASCII characters and ensure_ascii=True breaks output
CallResult.to_json_object() has a bug that adds non-JSON values
fix_steps: Test JSON output: hermes-call --json 'test' | jq . (should parse without errors)
Check types: hermes-call --json 'test' | jq 'keys' (should include required keys)
Verify escaping: hermes-call --json 'test' | od -c | head (should have \u escapes for special chars)
Test with Unicode: echo 'unicode: 你好' | hermes-call - --json | jq .answer
Verify CallResult.to_json_object() in core.py uses json.dumps with ensure_ascii=False and separators=(',', ':')
change_robust: json.dumps() is robust; ensure_ascii=False and separators are explicitly set; all CallResult fields are JSON-serializable (bool, int, str, list, dict, None)
detection_method: Run hermes-call --json 'test'; capture stdout; parse with json.loads(); verify keys 'ok', 'answer', 'model', 'duration_ms', 'session_cleaned', 'exit_code' are present

what_it_checks: Timeout value is positive and not zero or negative
failure_symptom: hermes-call exits with code 2; error 'timeout must be greater than 0'
likely_causes: User passed --timeout 0
User passed --timeout -1
Float parsing issue (--timeout 0.0001 might be treated as zero)
fix_steps: Use positive timeout: hermes-call --timeout 30 'prompt'
Default timeout is 600s: hermes-call 'prompt' (no --timeout needed)
For very fast runs: hermes-call --timeout 10 'prompt'
change_robust: Validation is done in options_from_namespace() before CallOptions is created; parser.error() ensures exit code 2 for user error
detection_method: Parse --timeout argument; check if timeout_seconds <= 0; call parser.error() if invalid; exit code 2

what_it_checks: Max iterations for --until-done is positive and > 0
failure_symptom: hermes-call exits with code 2; error 'max-iterations must be greater than 0'
likely_causes: User passed --max-iterations 0
User passed --max-iterations -1
fix_steps: Use positive value: hermes-call --until-done --max-iterations 8 'prompt'
Default is 8: hermes-call --until-done 'prompt' (no --max-iterations needed)
change_robust: Validation in options_from_namespace(); parser.error() ensures clean exit code 2
detection_method: Parse --max-iterations argument; check if max_iterations <= 0; call parser.error() if invalid; exit code 2

what_it_checks: Conflicting flag combinations are rejected with clear error message
failure_symptom: hermes-call exits with code 2; error 'X and Y cannot be used together'
likely_causes: User passed both --ask and --toolsets (both specify toolset)
User passed both --cd and --sandbox (both control cwd)
User passed --keep without --sandbox (--keep is meaningless without sandbox)
fix_steps: Use one toolset option: hermes-call --ask 'prompt' (or --toolsets xyz, not both)
Use one cwd option: hermes-call -C /path 'prompt' (or --sandbox, not both)
Use --keep only with --sandbox: hermes-call --sandbox --keep 'prompt'
change_robust: Validation in options_from_namespace(); clear error messages for each conflict; parser.error() ensures predictable exit code 2
detection_method: Check for invalid combinations: --ask + --toolsets, --cd + --sandbox, --keep + (not --sandbox); call parser.error() if found; exit code 2

what_it_checks: Live doctor check can reach the model and get a response within timeout
failure_symptom: doctor --live returns live_answer=False; exit_code != 0 or answer is empty; error is 'no final response produced' or timeout
likely_causes: API key or model credentials are invalid or expired
Model provider (xAI, OpenAI, local vLLM) is not reachable (network issue)
Model is overloaded or slow (timeout hit)
Default model is not set (config.yaml has no model.default)
fix_steps: Test model directly: hermes -z 'hello' (should return answer within seconds)
Check auth: hermes auth list (should show valid credentials)
Check model: hermes config model.default (should not be null)
Test network: ping api.x.ai or ping api.openai.com (depending on provider)
Run doctor --live with longer timeout: hermes-call doctor --live --timeout 300
Check ~/.hermes/.env for API keys: grep -i 'key\|token' ~/.hermes/.env | head
change_robust: Live check is optional (doctor without --live succeeds); live check has configurable timeout; detects both exit code failure and empty answer; returns detailed reason
detection_method: Run hermes --ask -z 'List files...' with timeout 120s; check exit code 0; check answer is not empty

what_it_checks: Process group is successfully terminated after timeout via SIGTERM and SIGKILL
failure_symptom: Process remains after hermes-call exits; orphaned hermes process in ps; exit code is 124 but process group survives; resource leak
likely_causes: os.killpg() fails due to permission or process already exited before kill
SIGTERM is not delivered (process in uninterruptible sleep, kernel bug)
Hermes ignores SIGTERM and requires SIGKILL
Process spawns daemon child that detaches from process group
fix_steps: Test manual termination: hermes -z 'import time; time.sleep(100)' &; sleep 1; kill -TERM $!; sleep 1; ps aux | grep 'hermes -z'
Test with strace: strace -e kill,signal hermes-call --timeout 1 'import time; time.sleep(100)' 2>&1 | grep -i kill
Check process group: bash -c 'hermes -z "sleep 100" & sleep 0.5; ps -o pid,pgid -p $!' (should have same pgid)
Verify SIGKILL fallback: in core.py, _terminate_process_group() does SIGTERM then SIGKILL
change_robust: Code uses start_new_session=True to create process group; os.killpg(pid, SIGTERM) terminates group; fallback to SIGKILL after timeout; detects and logs errors
detection_method: Run hermes with infinite loop; timeout after 2 seconds; check stderr for 'process group' termination messages; verify process is gone: ps aux | grep hermes

what_it_checks: API keys and tokens are redacted from diagnostic output in sanitize_diagnostic()
failure_symptom: Diagnostic output printed to stderr contains raw API key or Bearer token; secret is exposed in logs or terminal history
likely_causes: SECRET_PATTERNS regex does not match new key format (e.g., 'api-key' with hyphen)
Bearer token format changed (new prefix)
Secret appears in non-matching location (not in assignment context)
Regex is not case-insensitive
fix_steps: Check patterns: grep -n 'SECRET_PATTERNS' src/cluxion_hermes_call/core.py
Test redaction: hermes-call 'test' 2>&1 | grep -i 'api.*key\|token\|secret\|bearer' (should not appear)
Test with fake prompt: hermes-call 'api_key=secret123' 2>&1 | grep -c secret123 (should be 0)
If pattern missing, add to SECRET_PATTERNS: re.compile(r'new_pattern')
change_robust: Multiple regex patterns match common secret formats (api_key, api-key, token, secret, password, bearer); case-insensitive; replaces with '[redacted]'; prompt is also redacted explicitly
detection_method: Create fake diagnostic with 'api_key=secret123'; call sanitize_diagnostic(); check 'secret123' is replaced with '[redacted]'

what_it_checks: Python version is >= 3.11 (required by cluxion-hermes-call-cli)
failure_symptom: ImportError or SyntaxError on from __future__ import annotations; type hints fail to parse; exit code during import
likely_causes: User has Python 3.10 installed
Python 3.11 is installed but not on PATH (using system Python 3.8)
Virtual environment is using wrong Python version
fix_steps: Check current Python: python --version (should be Python 3.11+)
Use specific Python: python3.11 -m hermes-call 'prompt'
Install Python 3.11+: brew install python@3.11 (macOS) or apt install python3.11 (Linux)
Use virtual environment: python3.11 -m venv venv && source venv/bin/activate && pip install cluxion-hermes-call-cli
Check cluxion-hermes-call-cli was installed for right Python: pip show cluxion-hermes-call-cli | grep Location
change_robust: Check can be done at import time; pyproject.toml declares requires-python = '>=3.11'; package metadata will reject installation on Python < 3.11
detection_method: Run python --version; parse major.minor; verify >= 3.11

what_it_checks: Hermes plugin entry point is correctly registered via entry-points in pyproject.toml
failure_symptom: hermes call doctor returns 'unrecognized command'; error 'call is not a known subcommand'; plugin is not installed
likely_causes: cluxion-hermes-call-cli is not installed in the same Python environment as Hermes
Hermes plugin discovery does not see entry-point (setup.py/pyproject.toml metadata issue)
Hermes was installed before cluxion-hermes-call-cli (needs reinstall or pip install --force-reinstall)
plugin.py has syntax error (register() function fails)
fix_steps: Verify installation: pip show cluxion-hermes-call-cli (should be installed)
Check entry-point: grep -A 2 'entry-points.*hermes_agent.plugins' pyproject.toml
Reinstall plugin: pip uninstall cluxion-hermes-call-cli && pip install cluxion-hermes-call-cli
Test plugin: hermes call 'hello' (should work; if not, try hermes call doctor)
Check Hermes plugin list: hermes call ... (no direct list, but any hermes call command should work if plugin loaded)
change_robust: Entry-point is specified in pyproject.toml; Hermes plugin discovery is automatic on install; can be tested by running any hermes call subcommand
detection_method: Check hermes call doctor runs without error; verify plugin.register() is called; check Hermes loads cluxion-hermes-call-cli as plugin
